objectives: Conjugate regular and common irregular verbs in the present subjunctive
Use subjunctive after expressions of desire, doubt, emotion, and recommendation
Recognize the WEIRDO triggers (Wishes, Emotion, Impersonal, Recommendations, Doubt/Denial, Ojalá)
Distinguish indicative from subjunctive in natural speech

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Profesora
dialogue_blocks.text: Quiero que todos estudien el subjuntivo esta semana.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I want everyone to study the subjunctive this week.

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Estudiante
dialogue_blocks.text: Espero que no sea tan difícil como parece.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I hope it's not as difficult as it seems.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [esˈpeɾo ke no ˈsea tan diˈfiθil ˈkomo paˈɾeθe]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Profesora
dialogue_blocks.text: Dudo que lo dominen en un día, pero con práctica lo lograrán.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I doubt you'll master it in one day, but with practice you'll get it.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [ˈðuðo ke lo ðoˈminen en un ˈðia ‖ ˈpeɾo kon ˈpɾaktika]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Estudiante
dialogue_blocks.text: ¿Es posible que lleguemos a usarlo de forma natural?
dialogue_blocks.translation: Is it possible that we'll come to use it naturally?
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [es poˈsiβle ke ʝeˈɣemos a uˈsaɾlo ðe ˈfoɾma natuˈɾal]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Profesora
dialogue_blocks.text: Absolutamente. Recomiendo que practiquen con nativos siempre que puedan.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Absolutely. I recommend you practice with natives whenever you can.

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Estudiante
dialogue_blocks.text: Me alegra que nos explique todo con tanto detalle.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I'm glad you explain everything to us in such detail.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [me aˈleɣɾa ke nos ekspliˈke ˈtoðo kon ˈtanto deˈtaʝe]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Profesora
dialogue_blocks.text: No creo que haya una forma más efectiva que la inmersión total.
dialogue_blocks.translation: I don't think there is a more effective way than total immersion.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [no ˈkɾeo ke ˈaʝa ˈuna ˈfoɾma mas efeˈktiβa ke la inmeɾˈsjon toˈtal]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Estudiante
dialogue_blocks.translation: Hopefully we'll have more classes like this one!

srs_flashcards.back: Quiero que tú vayas (I want you to go)
No creo que + subjunctive (I don't think that...)

exercises.instruction: Escoge la forma correcta del subjuntivo (Choose the correct subjunctive form)
exercises.question: My mom wants me to call her every day. Which is correct?

options.text: Mi mamá quiere que la llame todos los días.
Mi mamá quiere que la llamo todos los días.
Mi mamá quiere que la llamé todos los días.
Mi mamá quiere yo llamo todos los días.

feedback.correct: ¡Perfecto! 'Quiere que' triggers the subjunctive — 'llame' (yo subjunctive of llamar). Two different subjects require que + subjunctive.
feedback.incorrect: 'Querer que' always triggers the subjunctive. With two different subjects (mom and me), the structure is: quiere que + subjunctive. 'Llame' is the yo present subjunctive of llamar.

exercises.instruction: Une el disparador con la categoría WEIRDO correcta (Match trigger to WEIRDO category)
Completa con la forma correcta del subjuntivo (Complete with the correct subjunctive form)

dialogue.speaker: Jefa
dialogue.text: Necesito que _____ el informe antes del viernes. (terminar — tú)

dialogue.speaker: Empleado
dialogue.text: Claro. ¿Es posible que me _____ más tiempo si hay algún problema? (dar — usted)

dialogue.speaker: Jefa
dialogue.text: Dudo que _____ necesario, pero lo consideraremos. (ser — ello)

dialogue.speaker: Empleado
dialogue.text: Ojalá que todo _____ bien esta semana. (salir)

speaking_rubric.title: Dar consejos y expresar deseos (Give advice and express wishes)
speaking_rubric.scenario: A friend is starting a new job. Give them advice using subjunctive expressions — what you hope, what you recommend, what's important.
speaking_rubric.prompt: Dale consejos a un amigo que empieza un trabajo nuevo. Usa el subjuntivo.

scoring_criteria.description: WEIRDO trigger expressions and advice vocabulary

sample_response.text: Espero que te vaya muy bien en tu nuevo trabajo. Es importante que llegues puntual los primeros días. Recomiendo que escuches mucho y hagas muchas preguntas. ¡Ojalá que te encante!
sample_response.ipa: [esˈpeɾo ke te ˈβaɣa muj ˈβjen ‖ es impoɾˈtante ke ˈʝeɣes punˈtwal]

cultural_notes.formality: The subjunctive is more present in formal registers — business correspondence, political speech, and polite requests. In casual Mexico City speech, speakers sometimes drop it in rapid conversation, but it's essential for educated expression.
cultural_notes.gestures: Raising one finger while saying 'es importante que' signals emphasis and authority.
cultural_notes.regional_variations: Mexican Spanish uses the present subjunctive frequently. 'Ojalá' (from Arabic 'inshallah') is universally used across all Spanish dialects and is always followed by the subjunctive. Puerto Rican Spanish uses the same subjunctive structures with the same triggers.
